introduction.p: Surveyors are trying to determine the height of a hill relative to sea level. First, they choose a point to take an initial measurement with a sextant that shows the angle of elevation from the ground to the peak of the hill is 19^\circ. Next, they move 1000 feet closer to the hill, staying at the same elevation relative to sea level, and find that the angle of elevation has increased to 25^\circ, as pictured in the given diagram. We let h represent the height of the hill relative to the two measurements, and x represent the distance from the second measurement location to the center of the hill that lies directly under the peak.

description.p: ADD ALT TEXT TO THIS IMAGE

statement.p: Using the right triangle with the 25^\circ angle, find an equation that relates x and h.

solution.p: Using the right triangle with the 25^\circ angle, the opposite side is h and the adjacent side is x, so \tan(25^\circ) = \dfrac{h}{x}.

statement.p: Using the right triangle with the 19^\circ angle, find a second equation that relates x and h.

solution.p: Using the right triangle with the 19^\circ angle, the opposite side is h and the adjacent side is x + 1000, so \tan(19^\circ) = \dfrac{h}{x+1000}.

statement.p: Our work in (a) and (b) results in a system of two equations in the two unknowns x and h. Solve each of the two equations for h and then substitute appropriately in order to find a single equation in the variable x.

solution.p:
From (a), h = x\tan(25^\circ). From (b), h = (x+1000)\tan(19^\circ). Setting equal:
x\tan(25^\circ) &= (x+1000)\tan(19^\circ) x\tan(25^\circ) - x\tan(19^\circ) &= 1000\tan(19^\circ) x(\tan(25^\circ) - \tan(19^\circ)) &= 1000\tan(19^\circ) x &= \dfrac{1000\tan(19^\circ)}{\tan(25^\circ) - \tan(19^\circ)}.

statement.p: Solve the equation from (c) to find the exact value of x and determine an approximate value accurate to 3 decimal places.

solution.p: The exact value is x = \dfrac{1000\tan(19^\circ)}{\tan(25^\circ) - \tan(19^\circ)}. Numerically, x \approx 3412.527 feet.

statement.p: Use your preceding work to solve for h exactly, plus determine an estimate accurate to 3 decimal places.

solution.p: Using h = x\tan(25^\circ) with x \approx 3412.527: h \approx 3412.527 \cdot \tan(25^\circ) \approx 1591.287 feet.

statement.p: If the surveyors' initial measurements were taken from an elevation of 78 feet above sea level, how high above sea level is the peak of the hill?

solution.p: If the initial measurements were taken from 78 feet above sea level, the peak of the hill is approximately 1591.287 + 78 \approx 1669 feet above sea level.
